verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 encodes TGN46 (also known as TGN38 in rodents), a type-I single-pass transmembrane protein that cycles constitutively between the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and early/recycling endosomes. Its steady-state localization is overwhelmingly in the TGN; only a very minor transient fraction passes through the plasma membrane as part of its recycling itinerary, but this passage is extremely brief and not a stable surface pool. Surface biotinylation studies confirm it is largely absent from the cell surface at steady state. It does not qualify as a stable surface receptor. No documented cancer/stress outer-leaflet pool, no lysosomal exocytosis evidence, no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering story, no germline/developmental restricted surface display. The transient PM passage during recycling is too brief to constitute a targetable surface pool.
